task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function accurately calculates the median in both odd and even length lists
trace: The function `median` takes a list of numbers as input and returns the median value. It first checks if the list is empty, in which case it returns `None`. Then, it sorts the list and calculates the middle index. If the length of the list is even, it returns the average of the two middle values; otherwise, it returns the middle value as a float. The function ensures readability by using clear variable names and handling edge cases appropriately.
